content.full_text:
FPPC

MAR 6 8 13 AM '90                    RICHARD WOOLSTRUM
                                     18 SOUTH MAIN STREET
                                     LODI, CALIFORNIA 95240
                                     FEBRUARY 26, 1990

                    FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
                    LEGAL DIVISION
                    P.O. BOX 807
                    SACRAMENTO, CA 95804


                    TO WHOM IT MAY CONCERN,
                         THIS MORNING, I WAS IN AND TOURED
                    TO LINDA HOURENUX REGARDING SOME PUBLIC
                    COMMENTARY ADS THAT HAVE BEEN RUNNING
                    UNDER MY NAME, RICHARD WOOLSTRUM, AND
                    GOLDEN ERA HOTEL , A PROPERTY THAT
                    IS OWNED BY GLORY PROPERTIES, INC..
                    I OWN 100% OF STOCK IN GLORY
                    PROPERTIES, INC.. THE PUBLIC COMMENTARY ADS HAVE PROVEN VERY
                    EFFECTIVE IN PROMOTING GOLDEN
                    ERA HOTEL. SINCE WE HAVE
                    STARTED RUNNING THESE COMMENTARIES,

                                             PAGE 1 OF 13 PAGES

[This image shows a blank lined notebook or notepad page with green horizontal lines on a cream/off-white background. There is a red/pink vertical line running down the center of the page, creating a margin. The page appears to be empty with no visible text written on it.]

THE HOTEL GROSS HAS INCREASED
BETWEEN $1,000 TO $2,000 PER MONTH.
WE BELIEVE THESE ADS ARE LARGELY
RESPONSIBLE FOR THE INCREASE, AS
THEY HAVE RESULTED IN TREMENDOUS
POSITIVE PUBLIC RESPONSE. IN FACT,
THIS LETTER AND ITS INQUIRIES TODAY
TO ITS. MOUREAUX ARE LARGELY DUE
TO THIS RESPONSE. I AM CONSTANTLY
BEING APPROACHED AND ENCOURAGED OR
REQUESTED TO RUN FOR COUNTY SUPER-
VISOR'S OFFICE, AND, AS OF THIS DATE,
I HAVE DECIDED TO TAKE OUT MY
CANDIDACY PAPERS SOMETIME NEXT
WEEK.

I SHOWED THE ENCLOSED FUND
COMMENTARIES TO MS. MOUREAUX
AND ASKED HER IF I COULD CONTINUE
THEM AFTER MY CANDIDACY IS DECLARED.
SHE TOOK COPIES OF 15-20 PAST
COMMENTARIES INTO A BACK OFFICE,
WHERE I ASSUME SHE DISCUSSED

PAGE 2 OF 13 PAGES

[The image shows a blank lined notebook page with faint, illegible handwritten text throughout. The text is too faint and unclear to accurately transcribe with certainty. The page has horizontal green lines and a vertical red margin line down the center.]

HIT QUESTION WITH ANOTHER LEGAL
ADVISOR. WHEN SHE RETURNED, SHE
GAVE THE FOLLOWING ANSWERS TO
HIT ASSOCIATE AND ME:
    IF I REMOVE ITY NAME AND
FACE FROM THE COMMENTARIES, IT
WOULD BE ALRIGHT TO CONTINUE THEM
THROUGHOUT THE PENDING CAMPAIGN
WITHOUT DECLARING THEM AS CAMPAIGN-
RELATED. IF I CHOSE NOT TO REMOVE
ITY NAME AND FACE FROM THE COM-
MENTARIES, I WOULD HAVE TO DECLARE
THEM AS POLITICAL ADS, INCLUDING THE
NAME OF ITY POLITICAL CAMPAIGN AND
CAMPAIGN MANAGER. GLORY PROPERTIES,
INC. COULD PAY A MAXIMUM OF $1,000
PER ELECTION, AND AFTER THAT, I
WOULD HAVE TO PAY FOR THE ARTICLES
WITH MY OWN HOMES OR CAMPAIGN
FUNDS.
    HIS HOUSEMAN STATED I NEEDED
TO PUT ALL ITY QUESTIONS IN WRITING

PAGE 3 OF 13 PAGES


AND SUBMIT THEM TO THE LEGAL DEPART-
MENT FOR WRITTEN ANSWERS. I FEEL
I NEED YOUR WRITTEN ANSWERS TO
THE FOLLOWING QUESTIONS, SO THERE
WILL BE NO CONFUSION, ON MY BEHALF,
CONCERNING ANY ISSUE THAT FALLS UNDER
PROPOSITIONS 68&73, OR ANY OTHER
PRE-EXISTING OR NEW UPDATE ON
CAMPAIGN DISCLOSURE PROVISIONS.
(TO SOME OF THE QUESTIONS I AM
PUTTING TO YOU, I HAVE NOTED
MS. HOUBRENIX'S VERBAL ANSWERS,
WHEN I QUESTIONED HER VERBALLY)
REGARDING THE FOLLOWING QUESTIONS,
PLEASE KEEP IN MIND THAT THESE
COMMENTARIES HAVE BEEN RUNNING
FOR SEVERAL MONTHS AND HAVE
BEEN SUCCESSFULLY USED TO ADVERTISE
THE GOLDEN ERA HOTEL, AND THERE
ARE NO INTENTIONS TO CHANGE COM-
MENTARY CONTENTS TO SUPPORT
RICHARD WOOLSTRUM BY NAME, OR

PAGE 4 OF 13 PAGES

[The image shows a blank lined notebook page with no visible text written on it. The page has horizontal blue/teal lines for writing, a vertical pink/red margin line on the left side, and appears to be cream or off-white colored paper.]

TO OPPOSE THE OPPOSITION BY NAME.
THE STYLE OF FUTURE COMMENTARY ADS IS
NOT PLANNED TO BE DIFFERENT FROM THE
STYLE OF THOSE PUBLISHED IN THE PAST.
1. IF THE COMMENTARIES ARE PAID
FOR BY GLORY PROPERTIES CORPORATION,
AND CONSIDERED BY THE CORPORATION TO
BE A BUSINESS AD, AFTER I DECLARE
ITS CANDIDACY, WILL THE ADS BE
CONSIDERED POLITICAL AND A DONATION
BY THE CORPORATION TO ITS CAMPAIGN,
SUBJECT TO A $50 DOLLAR LIMIT
PER ELECTION ? (MS. MOUREAUX
THOUGHT THE ANSWER TO THIS WOULD
BE "YES.")
WILL THE COMMENTARIES, AT THAT
TIME, ALSO NEED AN ADDITIONAL POLITICAL
DISCLOSURE SUCH AS, "PAID FOR BY
FRIENDS OF RICHARD WOODSTREAM" AND
THE I.D. NUMBER ? ( AGAIN MS.
MOUREAUX THOUGHT "YES.")
2. IF THE ADS ARE CONSIDERED AS

PAGE 5 OF 13 PAGES


A DONATION, WHAT CAN GLORY PROPER-
TIES CORPORATION (GOLDEN ERA HOTEL)
SPEND ON THIS TYPE OF AD AFTER
H4 DECLARATION OF CANDIDACY ?
$1,000.00 OR WHAT ?
3. GLORY PROPERTIES, INC OWES
RICHARD WOOLSTRUM WELL OVER
$100,000.00. COULD GLORY PROPERTIES,
INC CONTINUE TO PAY FOR CON-
TENTARIE'S OVER THE $1,000.00
(OR WHATEVER YOU SAY TO QUESTIONED)
LIMIT, AND DEDUCT THE EWES HOMES
FROM HOMES OWED TO RICHARD
WOOLSTRUM? (MS. HOUREAUX
THOUGHT NOT.)
4. IF THE ANSWER TO QUESTION #3
IS "NO", CAN GLORY PROPERTIES, INC.
CONTINUE TO RUN ADS IN THE 3
NEWSPAPERS CURRENTLY CONTRACTED
WITH FOR THE BALANCE OF THEIR
6 MONTHS CONTRACT ? THE LAST ONE
EXPIRES IN AUGUST. (MS. HOUREAUX
SAID "YES" IF BILWORD WOOLSTRUM.
PAGE 6 OF 13 PAGES


WILL PAY FOR ADS PERSONALLY, OR HAVE
THE CAMPAIGN PAY THE HOMES AND
DECLARE "PAID FOR BY . . . . AND THE
I.D. NUMBER.)
5. IF THE ANSWER TO QUESTION[4]
IS "YES", CAN THE COMMENTARY CHANGE
ITS FORMAT AND INCLUDE POLITICAL
OPPOSITION AND SUPPORT OF CANDIDATE?
(THIS QUESTION WAS NOT ASKED MS.
HOUREUX.)
6. DO GLORY PROPERTIES, INC ADS,
OR ANY OTHER EXPENSES RELATED TO
THE COMMENTARY ADS THAT RAN PRIOR
TO RICHARD WOOLSTRUM'S DECLARING
CANDIDACY, NEED TO BE DECLARED ON
FPPC STATEMENTS? (MS. HOUREUX
SAID "NO".)
7. GLORY PROPERTIES, INC.
(GOLDEN ERA NOTE)—IN TRYING TO
COMPLY WITH FPPC REQUIREMENT
AND STILL RUN PUBLIC COMMENTARIES
AS IN PREVIOUSLY PUBLISHED FORMAT—

PAGE 7 OF 13 PAGES

[The image shows a blank lined notebook page with no visible text content. The page has horizontal lines in light blue/cyan color and a vertical red line running down the middle, typical of standard notebook paper. The page appears to be empty with no written text to transcribe.]

WANTS TO KNOW, IF THE PHOTO OF
RICHARD WOOLSTRUMS FACE WAS REMOVED
AND THE NAME "RICHARD WOOLSTRUM" DELETED
FROM THE AD, COULD GLORY PROPERTIES,
INC. CONTINUE TO RUN THE COMMENTARY
AD FOR THE HOTEL AS NON-POLITICALLY-
RELATED ? (HS. MOUREAUX SAID "YES")
8. COULD GLORY PROPERTIES INC.
(GOLDEN ERA HOTEL) RUN THE SAME
FORMAT OF COMMENTARIES AS IN THE
PAST WITH JOANI WOOLSTRUM, EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR OF GLORY PROPERTIES, INC.,
AS AUTHOR—NOT MENTIONING RICHARD
WOOLSTRUMS NAME OR ANY OTHER
POLITICAL NAME — AND NOT BE
CONSIDERED POLITICALLY-RELATED,
BY THE FRPC, TO MY PROPOSED 1990
CAMPAIGN ? (IN KEEPING WITH THE
ANSWER TO #7, HS. MOUREAUX IMPLIED
THE ANSWER WAS "YES")
9. IS THERE A LIMIT ON AMOUNTS
OF MONIES OR IN-KIND SERVICES AN

PAGE 8 OF 13 PAGES


INDIVIDUAL OR CORPORATION MAY LOAN
TO A CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE?
(MS. MOUREAUX WAS NOT ASKED THIS
SPECIFIC QUESTION.)
10. IF A CAMPAIGN COMMITTEE IS
UNABLE TO PAY BACK THE FULL AMOUNT
BORROWED, AND THE AMOUNT REMAINING
DUE EXCEEDED THE LEGAL CAMPAIGN
DONATION LIMIT, AND THE LENDER DELIVED
TO FORGIVE THE DEBT, HOW WOULD IT
BE HANDLED UNDER PRESENT FPPC
REGULATIONS? THIS HAS BEEN STANDARD
PRACTICE IN THE PAST BY POLITICAL
CANDIDATES. (MS. MOUREAUX WAS NOT
ASKED THIS QUESTION.)
11. IF A PAST CANDIDATE HAD
PERSONALLY LOANED HIS CAMPAIGN
COMMITTEE HOME'S, AND THEN LOST
THE ELECTION, AND THE CANDIDATE
FORMS A NEW CAMPAIGN COMMITTEE
FOR ANOTHER ELECTION, CAN THE
NEW CAMPAIGN COMMITTEE REQUIRE

PAGE 9 OF 13 PAGES


THE DEGREE OF TRUTH.

PART ONE: THE DEGREE OF TRUTH IN LANGUAGE.

CAN YOU POINT OUT THE DEGREE TO WHICH EACH OF THE FOLLOWING STATEMENTS IS TRUE?

1. AUDACIOUS BIRD PEOPLE MAKE PEOPLE NOT FULLY APPRECIATIVE OF THE DEGREE TO WHICH EACH STATEMENT IS TRUE?

2. HAS THE DEGREE TO WHICH EACH IS TRUE BEEN FULLY APPRECIATED AS PERHAPS THE MOST IMPORTANT QUESTION?

IF IT WERE NOT FOR PEOPLE THE EARTH WOULD BE TOO DANGEROUS. HIS PART AN EFFECTIVE PART TO PLAY.

A DOCTOR EFFECTIVE PART AN EFFECTIVE PART TO PLAY.

[illegible] DOCTOR'S EFFECT [illegible] PART TO PLAY.

EXCEPTA [illegible] CAUSE IS CALLED ROLE. THE PART (or [illegible] FURNITURE) SATISFIES THE PART AND HARD INDUSTRY REQUIRES THE PART AND HARD INDUSTRY SATISFIES THE PART.

NL THE

ESSENCE

PACER NATURE ARE HARD INDUSTRY SATISFIES THE PART AND HARD INDUSTRY SATISFIES THE PART.

PEACE NATURE ARE HARD INDUSTRY SATISFIES THE PART. SATISFIES THE PART.

TASK ARE HARD INDUSTRY AND HARD INDUSTRY SATISFIES THE PART.

APODIOSIS SUPPORTS 89.5 SUPPORTED BY SUPPORTERS OF TRUTH SUPPORTED BY SUPPORTERS OF TRUTH WITH UNEQUIVOCAL SUPPORT OF AN ADVANCED LEAD.

HAS THE HARD [illegible] AND [illegible] SUPPORTED

SAGE IMPORTANCE SUPPORTED AS IMPORTANCE OF [illegible] HARD PROGRESS WITH HARD SUPPORT OF LEADING LEADER'S IMPORTANCE.

USE DAYS HARD DAYS IMPORTANCE IMPORTANCE IMPORTANCE DAYS

AUDITOR PROVIDED BUT OUTPUT BY [illegible] THE HARD DAYS

BE DEGREE ABOVE BEFORE [illegible] ABOVE ABOVE

PAGE 100/PAGES

sections.question: 1. If the commentaries are paid for by Glory Properties Corporation, and considered by the corporation to be a business ad, after I declare my candidacy, will the ads be considered political and a donation by the corporation to my campaign, subject to a set dollar limit per election? Will the commentaries, at that time, also need an additional political disclosure such as, "Paid for by Friends of Richard Woolstrum" and the I.D. number? 2. If the ads are considered as a donation, what can Glory Properties Corporation (Golden Era Hotel) spend on this type of ad after my declaration of candidacy? 3. Glory Properties, Inc owes Richard Woolstrum well over $100,000.00. Could Glory Properties, Inc continue to pay for commentaries over the $1,000.00 (or whatever you say to question #2) limit, and deduct the excess homes from homes owed to Richard Woolstrum? 4. If the answer to question #3 is "no", can Glory Properties, Inc continue to run ads in the 3 newspapers currently contracted with for the balance of their 6 months contract? 5. If the answer to question #4 is "yes", can the commentary change its format and include political opposition and support of candidates? 6. Do Glory Properties, Inc. ads, or any other expenses related to the commentary ads that ran prior to Richard Woolstrum's declaring candidacy, need to be declared on FPPC statements? 7. In trying to comply with FPPC requirements and still run public commentaries as in previously published format — wants to know, if the photo of Richard Woolstream's face was removed and the name "RICHARD WOOLSTREAM" deleted from the ad, could Glory Properties, Inc. continue to run the commentary ad for the hotel as non-politically-related? 8. Could Glory Properties, Inc. (Golden Era Hotel) run the same format of commentaries as in the past with Joani Woolstream, Executive Director of Glory Properties, Inc., as author—not mentioning Richard Woolstream's name or any other political name—and not be considered politically-related, by the FPPC, to my proposed 1990 campaign? 9. Is there a limit on amounts of homes or in-kind services an individual or corporation may loan to a candidate's committee? 10. If a campaign committee is unable to pay back the full amount borrowed, and the amount remaining due exceeded the legal campaign donation limit, and the lender decided to forgive the debt, how would it be handled under present FPPC regulations? 11. If a past candidate who personally loaned his campaign committee money, and then lost the election, and the candidate forms a new campaign committee for another election, can the new campaign committee acquire the debts of the previous campaign committee, in order to repay the candidate past homes loaned? 12. An elected candidate can only raise money in the 12 months prior to a coming election that affects his position or another election for which he has announced his candidacy. If this is true, let's assume I win in the primary. Can I continue after the primary to raise funds?
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor, Richard Woolestrom, who owns Golden Era Hotel through Glory Properties, Inc., seeks clarification on whether pre-existing business commentary advertisements can continue running after his declared candidacy for County Supervisor, and the extent to which they must be treated as political contributions subject to donation limits and disclosure requirements under Propositions 68 and 73.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document contains 12 specific questions from the requestor to FPPC. Ms. Moureaux (FPPC staff) provided some preliminary verbal answers, which are noted in parentheses by the requestor. The requestor explicitly asks for written answers to formalize these preliminary responses. Document quality is poor with many blank pages interrupting the text flow.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: 1. If the commentaries are paid for by Glory Properties Corporation, and considered by the corporation to be a business ad, after I declare my candidacy, will the ads be considered political and a donation by the corporation to my campaign, subject to a set dollar limit per election? Will the commentaries, at that time, also need an additional political disclosure such as, "Paid for by Friends of Richard Woolstrum" and the I.D. number? 2. If the ads are considered as a donation, what can Glory Properties Corporation (Golden Era Hotel) spend on this type of ad after my declaration of candidacy? 3. Glory Properties, Inc owes Richard Woolstrum well over $100,000.00. Could Glory Properties, Inc continue to pay for commentaries over the $1,000.00 (or whatever you say to question #2) limit, and deduct the excess homes from homes owed to Richard Woolstrum? 4. If the answer to question #3 is "no", can Glory Properties, Inc continue to run ads in the 3 newspapers currently contracted with for the balance of their 6 months contract? 5. If the answer to question #4 is "yes", can the commentary change its format and include political opposition and support of candidates? 6. Do Glory Properties, Inc. ads, or any other expenses related to the commentary ads that ran prior to Richard Woolstrum's declaring candidacy, need to be declared on FPPC statements? 7. In trying to comply with FPPC requirements and still run public commentaries as in previously published format — wants to know, if the photo of Richard Woolstream's face was removed and the name "RICHARD WOOLSTREAM" deleted from the ad, could Glory Properties, Inc. continue to run the commentary ad for the hotel as non-politically-related? 8. Could Glory Properties, Inc. (Golden Era Hotel) run the same format of commentaries as in the past with Joani Woolstream, Executive Director of Glory Properties, Inc., as author—not mentioning Richard Woolstream's name or any other political name—and not be considered politically-related, by the FPPC, to my proposed 1990 campaign? 9. Is there a limit on amounts of homes or in-kind services an individual or corporation may loan to a candidate's committee? 10. If a campaign committee is unable to pay back the full amount borrowed, and the amount remaining due exceeded the legal campaign donation limit, and the lender decided to forgive the debt, how would it be handled under present FPPC regulations? 11. If a past candidate who personally loaned his campaign committee money, and then lost the election, and the candidate forms a new campaign committee for another election, can the new campaign committee acquire the debts of the previous campaign committee, in order to repay the candidate past homes loaned? 12. An elected candidate can only raise money in the 12 months prior to a coming election that affects his position or another election for which he has announced his candidacy. If this is true, let's assume I win in the primary. Can I continue after the primary to raise funds?

embedding.summary: Richard Woolestrom requests written FPPC advice on the treatment of business commentary advertisements for Golden Era Hotel under campaign finance law after declaring candidacy for County Supervisor, including questions about donation limits, disclosure requirements, and related campaign finance issues.
